title: In Spirit and in Truth
speaker: Pastor Tito Lyro
source_files: In Spirit and in Truth - Pastor Tito Lyro - 1 John 2_18-27.txt

outline.title: Introduction: What Is Your Hope in Life and in Death?
subpoints: False teachers in John's congregation claimed their special knowledge guaranteed God's favor and included denying that Jesus was fully man and fully God
John says our hope is not in secret knowledge but in the whole Christ as taught in Scripture and applied by the Holy Spirit

Two main points (essentially the same thing said differently): those born from above are not deceived by false teaching; the Spirit and Word preserve the Christian in commitment to the Christ of the Bible
The biggest threat to the church is not Islam, secularism, or government but a bad theology of Christ

outline.title: 1. Those Born from Above Are Not Deceived by False Teaching
subpoints: Verses 18-19: the antichrists had been part of the visible church but had never been part of the invisible church of Christ — they had never been truly united to Christ

F.F. Bruce: 'Membership in a Christian society does not always imply that one belongs to the persevering saints; passage on the books of the local church does not necessarily carry with it passage in the Holy Book of Life'
John uses the same word for the antichrists going out as he used of Judas leaving the upper room — a deliberately echoing connection

Verse 19: 'If they had been of us, they would have continued with us' — God will preserve those who are truly his; John 6:37-40: 'All that the Father gives me will come to me, and of all he has given me I should lose nothing but raise it up at the last day'

outline.title: 2. The Anointing — The Holy Spirit Dwells in Every Believer
subpoints: Verse 20: 'You have an anointing from the Holy One and you know all things' — better translated: 'you all know' — John is not bringing new information but affirming what they already know through the Spirit

The anointing that comes from the Holy One (v.20) is the Holy Spirit: Isaiah 61:1 (Jesus was anointed by the Spirit); Acts 10:38 (God anointed Jesus with the Holy Spirit and power); 2 Corinthians 1:21-22 (believers have also been anointed)
In John's writings, Jesus is emphasized as the sender of the Spirit (John 16:13-14) — the Spirit always leads us to the real Jesus
Every true believer has the Spirit dwelling in them right now — God himself has made an abode in your heart

outline.title: 3. The Spirit and the Word Always Work Together
subpoints: Verses 24 and 27: what they heard from the beginning (the apostolic message — the Bible) and the anointing (Holy Spirit) together safeguard the believer against heresy concerning Christ
The Holy Spirit always works through the Word and never apart from the Word; to say 'I feel the Spirit leading me to something contrary to the Bible' is to follow a different spirit
John 4 promises the Spirit will lead the disciples into all truth — the fulfillment of this is the New Testament
Verse 27 does not mean the church needs no teachers; it means the church does not need anyone teaching anything contrary to what the Spirit has already revealed in Scripture about Christ

outline.title: 4. Three Tests and a Call to Self-Examination
subpoints: John has now given three tests for knowing that Christ is our Savior: am I obeying what God says in his Word? Do I love those whom Christ loves — my brothers? Do I believe in the Jesus of the Bible — both fully man and fully God?
The first thing to ask when evaluating a Christian teacher is not eloquence or credentials but: does he get Christ right? Does he get the Trinity right?
Anyone who claims a message from God not in the Bible is an antichrist — the Spirit and the Word are always together and the Word is complete
Personal call: do you believe in the Jesus of the Bible today, right now? The difference between eternal life with God and eternal life under his wrath is what you believe about this Jesus

summary:
Preaching from 1 John 2:18-27, Pastor Tito Lyro takes up John's confrontation with the antichrists — teachers who had been in the visible church and who claimed to possess special knowledge unavailable to ordinary believers, while denying that Jesus was fully man and fully God. The sermon presents two closely related points: those truly born from above will not be deceived by such false teaching, and the Holy Spirit and the Word of God together preserve the believer in commitment to the Christ of the Bible.

The antichrists' departure from the church exposed their true nature — they had never been united to Christ, however long they sat in the pews or however effectively they seemed to minister. John's use of the same word for their going out as he used of Judas leaving the upper room is deliberate and chilling. By contrast, those who are truly the Father's have been given to the Son, and the Son will lose none of them (John 6:37-40). The anointing every believer has received — the indwelling Holy Spirit — is the Spirit who leads to the real Jesus and who, working inseparably with the apostolic Word (the Scriptures), guards against every distortion of Christ.

The sermon presses toward a practical test: the first and most essential question about any teacher is whether he gets Christ right — fully man, fully God, the only Savior. The sermon closes with a personal and searching call: do you believe in this Jesus — the Christ of the Bible — right now? That belief is the difference between eternal life with God and eternal life under his wrath.
transcript:
## Introduction: What Is Your Hope?

What is your trust in? What is it that you really rely on? What is it that you count on for this life? What is it that you reach for when everything is closing in on you? What is your hope in life and in death? Where are you putting all your eggs?

These questions have a lot to do with what John is teaching us in this passage. False teachers — John calls them antichrists — were teaching this congregation that the most important thing was some special knowledge they alone possessed. And you should always be concerned when a teacher tells you they have something to tell you that nobody else knows and you can only get from them. That is exactly what these people were doing here.

They said that this special knowledge guaranteed God's favor. And this special knowledge included the teaching that Jesus was not what the Bible said he was. In the context of 1 John, the antichrists were denying that Jesus was fully man and fully God in one person.

John says, on the other hand, that our hope in life and in death is not in some secret knowledge. Our only hope is in the whole Christ as taught in the Scriptures and applied to us by the Holy Spirit.

I want to present this truth with two points. These two points are not entirely different ideas — they are essentially the same thing said differently. Sometimes it helps to say the same thing from different angles.

The first: those who are born from above — born again, given the Spirit of God — are not deceived by false teaching. There is nothing that can be said concerning Christ that will deceive those truly born from above.

The second: the Spirit of God and the Word of God preserve the Christian in his or her commitment to the Christ of the Bible.

## 1. Those Born from Above Are Not Deceived by False Teaching

Last week we saw that we live in a time marked by deception. In verses 18 and 19, John tells us we are in the last hour — the era between the first and second comings of Christ — and that we know this because antichrists are all over the place. Many antichrists throughout the history of the church point to that final antichrist who is yet to come.

These teachers were in the church and claimed to believe in a version of Jesus — they were not necessarily Jewish people who simply refused to believe in Christ. These were people who claimed to be Christians, who perhaps grew up in the church, and now continued to claim faith in some version of Christ, just not the Christ of the Bible.

And the biggest deception — John teaches us — is that the Christ of the Bible is not true. This is the biggest threat we face as a church of Jesus Christ. Not Islam. Not secularism. Not government. The biggest threat to the church of Jesus Christ is a bad theology of Christ. That is what John tells us here.

For today, we see that those who are born from above are not deceived by false teaching about Christ.

Look at verses 18 and 19: 'Little children, it is the last hour, and as you have heard, the antichrist is coming; even now many antichrists have come, by which we know it is the last hour. They went out from us, but they were not of us; for if they had been of us, they would have continued with us; but they went out that they might be made manifest that none of them were of us.'

These antichrists had been part of the visible church. They had been sitting in the pews — perhaps even elders or deacons or seemingly good teachers in the church. But though they had been part of the visible church, they had never been part of the invisible church of Christ. They had never truly been united to Christ.

F.F. Bruce, commenting on this passage, says: 'Membership in a Christian society does not always imply that one belongs to the persevering saints; passage on the books of the local church does not necessarily carry with it passage in the Holy Book of Life.'

These antichrists are proof that mere involvement in the local church does not make you a child of God. To become a child of God you must come to faith in the Christ of the Bible — in who he is and in what he has done for a sinner like yourself. Now, you cannot be a faithful Christian without being an active member of a local church — but that is a consequence of having been changed by the Spirit and having come to faith in Christ; it is not the cause.

It is interesting that in verse 19, John uses the same word to describe the antichrists going out of the church as he used of Judas leaving the upper room to betray Jesus. There were many words he could have used. He chose that one. In John 13:30: 'Having received the piece of bread, he then went out immediately, and it was night.' Judas had been with Jesus for at least two years. He healed the sick. He cast out demons. He proclaimed the gospel. People may have come to saving knowledge of Jesus Christ through his preaching — but Judas himself had never had his heart changed by that very gospel.

And John's explanation is clear: they went out from us but were not of us. For if they had been of us, they would have continued with us. Their departure was their unmasking. Once they left — once they were excommunicated or went their own way — you could see them for who they really were: deniers of the Christ of the Bible.

This is the Christian faith: not about some extraordinary beginning, but about a faithful ending. Remember the parable of the soils. The seed that falls on good soil grows slowly and steadily and over a lifetime produces fruit. That is the life of the Christian — a life of steady growth in Christ.

So let me ask you: where are you with your faith in Jesus Christ today? Not yesterday — tomorrow is not here yet. Are you believing in the right Jesus? Are you here but not of here? Jesus gives a very disturbing warning in Luke 12 — there is nothing covered that will not be revealed. Who are you today? If the mask were removed, would it still be you? Or would you find that all these years you have been lying about your faith to others and to yourself?

## 2. The Spirit and the Word Preserve the Christian

The Spirit of God and the Word of God together preserve the Christian in his commitment to the Christ of the Bible. Look at verse 20: 'But you have an anointing from the Holy One, and you know all things.'

The better translation behind this is: 'You all know' — John is not claiming they know everything about everything. He is affirming that they all know what he is talking about. He is not bringing anything new. He says the same in verse 24: 'Let that abide in you which you heard from the beginning.' He keeps connecting what he says with what they have always heard in the gospel of Jesus Christ.

The anointing that comes from the Holy One in verse 20 is the Holy Spirit. The Scriptures often speak of the Holy Spirit as the anointing. In Luke 4, Jesus quotes Isaiah 61 and says, 'The Spirit of the Lord is upon me because he has anointed me to preach the gospel to the poor.' In Acts 10:38, Peter says, 'God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power.' And in 2 Corinthians 1:21-22, Paul says that those who have been born again have received the Holy Spirit as an anointing from God.

The Holy One who sends the Spirit is Jesus Christ himself. In John's writings, John likes to emphasize that Jesus is the center, the sender of the Spirit. John 16:13-14: Jesus says he is going to send 'the one who will lead them into all truth' — the Holy Spirit. And the Holy Spirit leads us to the real Jesus.

Every true believer has the Holy Spirit dwelling in them right now. God himself has made an abode in your heart. He is present with you at this moment. And the Holy Spirit, together with the apostolic message — the Bible — will safeguard the believer against heresy concerning Christ.

Notice: John says that what they heard from the beginning abides in them (v.24) and that the anointing abides in them (v.27). What they heard from the beginning is the apostolic message. John joins the Holy Spirit with the Bible: together the Word and the Spirit keep the true believer from believing heresy about Jesus Christ. And that is important: the Holy Spirit always works with the Word, and never apart from the Word.

I know of people who say: 'I know what the Bible says concerning this. But I really feel the Spirit is leading me to something contrary to what the Bible says.' If a spirit is leading you to do something contrary to the Bible, it is not the Spirit of God — it is from the other side. The Spirit and the Word are always working together.

In John 4, Jesus promised his disciples that the Holy Spirit would guide them into all truth. The fulfillment of that promise is the New Testament. And John says here: this is the truth. Whatever contradicts it is falsehood. Jesus is the Christ. Jesus is the God-man. Whatever is contrary to that is a lie.

Now verse 27: 'You do not need anyone to teach you.' We need to compare Scripture with Scripture here. John cannot mean that the church does not need any teachers — he himself is teaching. One of the gifts Christ gave to his church in Ephesians 4 is teachers. One of the requirements to be an elder is that you are able to teach. So John does not mean that the church needs no teachers at all.

What John means is that the church does not need anyone teaching anything contrary to what the Spirit has already revealed in Scripture concerning Christ. He is speaking about the completeness of God's revelation. The antichrists were claiming they had special teaching that knowingly contradicted the apostles — and the church does not need that kind of teaching. He is speaking about the false teachers, not all teachers.

When we are trying to figure out whether to listen to a Christian teacher, the first thing we need to determine is not how eloquent he is, how easy to understand, or what his academic credentials are. The first thing is: does he get Christ right? Does he get the Trinity right? If he does not, the Spirit is not in him and he does not get the Bible correctly. In 1 Corinthians 2, Paul says the only way to know God is by the Spirit of God. If you do not have the Spirit of God, you do not get God as he reveals himself in the Bible.

So believing in the Trinity — Father, Son, and Holy Spirit, three persons in one God — is the absolutely non-negotiable minimum for one to teach in and be heard by the church. Anyone who claims to have a message from God that is not in the Bible is an antichrist. The Spirit of God does not dwell in such a person.

## Conclusion: Do You Believe in This Jesus?

John has now given us three tests to know that we know Christ is our Savior. First: am I obeying what God says in his Word? Second: do I love those whom Christ loves — the brethren? Third, today: do I believe in the Jesus of the Bible, who is both man and God and who is able to save to the ends of the earth?

Do you believe in that Jesus? It is the difference between eternal life with God and eternal life under the wrath of God, apart from his love. Do not leave today without making sure you believe in this Jesus.

Father, we thank you for your revelation of Jesus Christ. We pray that our faith would be in this Jesus — the one, the only one, revealed in your Word: your Son, the God and man, the Savior of the world. Grant us that kind of faith, we ask in Jesus' name. Amen.
